content: We can reframe language learning vocabulary acquisition as an iterative self-refinement loop inspired by LLM Self-Refine, anchored to the structured self-reflection memory mechanism of the Reflexion autonomous agent. Learners would not just memorize reflection-related vocabulary once, but iteratively test their usage in context, reflect on gaps between their output and native norms, and store corrected usage patterns in a dynamic personal memory bank that improves over time.
